title: When I Cannot See
blurb: A prayer for the times we are walking in the dark. It does not ask for the whole road to light up, only for enough light for the next step, and a hand to hold when we cannot stand on our own.

title: Mercy Has A Way
blurb: For the tired ones standing at the edge of giving up. Mercy has a way of finding them there, of walking them back, and of knowing their name even when no rescue feels loud enough.

title: Easy Hallelujahs
blurb: For anyone who cannot fake the easy kind of praise. This is worship pulled out of real pain, the hard won hallelujah that costs something, sung by someone who keeps coming back to God anyway.

title: Signals
blurb: Written facing a body that is failing, and the quiet fear of a mind that might follow. It insists that who we are in God outlasts whatever we lose, that something still gets through even when the body will not reply.

title: What Love Becomes
blurb: A song about what love turns into long after the romance settles. The dishes, the bills, the choosing each other again. Not a spark in the dark, but a hand that keeps holding on.

title: I Asked You To Change Me
blurb: About the hard cost of a prayer that actually gets answered. Wanting to be made new, and then flinching as God begins to cut away the pride and the anger we did not really want to lose.

title: Before I Find Words
blurb: Sometimes God is closest before we find anything to say. This song sits in that silence, in the moment before the prayer, where He is already nearer to us than we are to our own thoughts.

title: Make Room In Me
blurb: A quiet act of surrender. Letting go of the plans and the control we cling to, and opening up space for God to do what He wants with a life that was never really ours.

title: Let The Morning Come
blurb: For the racing mind that cannot switch off. A gentle permission to set it all down, to stop carrying tomorrow tonight, and to trust that God is already waiting in a morning we cannot see yet.

title: I Still Look For You
blurb: An honest song about seeking God through doubt, anger, and the distance that quietly creeps in. Faith that feels far away, and a heart that keeps looking anyway.

title: I Am Not Forsaken
blurb: A song that holds onto faith when everything says to let go. Closed doors, injustice, the paper kingdoms that promise so much and deliver fear. It refuses to give fear the last word, and keeps believing it will see goodness in the land of the living.

title: Still Here Somehow
blurb: For anyone who has come through a grief they did not think they would survive. Barely breath by breath, still standing, still saying His name, kept by a grace that was never loud.

title: Good To Me Again
blurb: A testimony of steady faithfulness through the hard mornings, the bedsides, the fear for the people we love. Not every prayer answered, but never once abandoned.

title: Lay It Down Today
blurb: About the kind of worry that disguises itself as being responsible. The relief of finally naming it, handing it over, and stopping the long effort of carrying what was never ours to hold.

title: Surrender
blurb: A morning offering. The first breath of the day handed over before the world starts asking for things. No masks, no earning, just mercy met before the noise begins.

title: You Stay Close
blurb: Coming to God with empty hands and finding He stays near. Not because of anything we have earned, but because He holds us. A song about grace that does not depend on us getting it right.

title: I Am Dust
blurb: A song that holds frailty and worth in the same hand. Made of dust, and still not nothing. Small, and still not finished.

title: Road Beneath My Feet
blurb: A lifelong ache for somewhere to belong that finally lands on a quiet truth. Maybe home was never a place at all. Maybe home was always Him.
